iterations.hypothesis: USDC (listing 1) and USDE (listing 28) spread is mean-reverting; tick-based EWMA z-score with alpha=0.99, entry_z=1.0, close_z=0.2, max_hold=60s.
changes: Initial strategy: StablecoinStatArb based on NExchangeArb, entry_z=1.0, close_z=0.2, ewma_alpha=0.99, max_hold=60s
iterations.analysis: 90k+ fills in 3 days. Tick-based EWMA alpha=0.99 has half-life of only 22s at 3.16 ticks/sec. Fires constantly on noise. Systematic losses.
iterations.next_action: Increase entry_z to 2.5, set close_z=0.0 to exit only when spread crosses mean.

iterations.hypothesis: Higher entry_z=2.5 filters noise; close_z=0.0 ensures exit only on true mean crossing; max_hold 10min.
iterations.analysis: 79% improvement but still losing. Tick-based EWMA std too small (~0.22 bps) so z=2.5 fires at only 0.55 bps above mean. EWMA adapts in 22s, close fires on mean drift not spread reversion.
iterations.next_action: Replace tick-based EWMA with time-weighted EWMA (halflife=300s). Lock entry reference.

iterations.hypothesis: Time-weighted EWMA (halflife=300s) gives a meaningful z-score. Locked entry reference prevents false closes.
iterations.analysis: Breakthrough: PnL +4.175, pct_positive_buckets=1.0. 1716 round trips at +0.00243 each. Latency insensitive.
iterations.next_action: Sweep halflife, entry_z, close_z to optimize.

iterations.hypothesis: Lower close_z captures more reversion; shorter halflife and lower entry_z increase trade frequency.
iterations.analysis: 340% PnL improvement (4.175->18.38). Monotonic trends: close_z=-0.5 beats 0.0 everywhere; shorter halflife better; lower entry_z better. Optima suggest lying outside tested grid.

iterations.hypothesis: Continuing trend: even shorter halflife and lower entry_z; close_z=-1.0 may hold trades to deeper reversion for more edge per round trip.
iterations.analysis: 36% PnL improvement (18.38->25.01). close_z=-1.0 dominates close_z=-0.5 in EVERY parameter combination by ~5 PnL units — holding to deeper reversion consistently captures more spread. PnL/round-trip improved 35% (0.00344->0.00466) with same trade frequency. Halflife trend less clear: optimal at 90s with 60s and 120s close behind; halflife=30s is not better despite earlier trend — suggests diminishing returns below ~60s. entry_z=1.0 is optimal: 0.75 generates too many marginal trades (more fills, lower PnL/RT), 1.25+ too selective. close_z trend still monotonically decreasing — optima may lie further below -1.0. User hint: sweep size to find slippage breakeven.
iterations.next_action: Per user hint: sweep size=[1000,2000,5000,10000,20000,50000] at best params (halflife=90, entry_z=1.0, close_z=-1.0) to find where slippage starts degrading PnL/round-trip.

iterations.hypothesis: Per user request: sweep size=[1000,2000,5000,10000,20000,50000] at best params (halflife=90, entry_z=1.0, close_z=-1.0) to find where slippage starts degrading PnL/round-trip.
changes: Remote sweep: size=[1000,2000,5000,10000,20000,50000], all other params fixed at iter 5 best
iterations.analysis: Strategy is capacity-constrained, not slippage-constrained. Fill count collapses with size: 21430 at size=1000 → 72 at size=10000 (99.7% drop). The risk_averse queue model places us last in queue; larger orders rarely fill at the taker prices we target. Per-RT PnL artificially rises with size (only the most extreme spreads trigger fills) but total PnL degrades sharply. size=1000 maximizes total PnL (25.01). size=50000 goes negative (6 fills, adverse selection on the rare fills that do happen). Confirmed: size=1000 is the capacity ceiling for this strategy.
iterations.next_action: Size confirmed at 1000. Continue optimizing close_z (trend still monotonic: 0.0 > -0.5 > -1.0) — try close_z=[-1.5,-2.0,-2.5] at best params. Also test max_position=[5,10,20] to allow more concurrent positions and increase frequency.

iterations.hypothesis: Deeper close_z may capture more reversion per trade; higher max_position may allow more concurrent positions for higher frequency.
changes: Remote sweep: close_z=[-1.0,-1.5,-2.0,-2.5], max_position=[5,10,20], all other params fixed at best
iterations.analysis: Two decisive findings: (1) max_position is completely irrelevant — identical results at 5/10/20. The strategy only opens one position at a time (both legs must be flat before entry), so position stacking never occurs. (2) close_z=-1.0 is the total-PnL optimum. Deeper thresholds reduce fill count faster than they improve per-trade edge: close_z=-1.5 halves fills but only reduces PnL by 19%. Hold time analysis confirms max_hold_ns is not binding: 97.4% of trades close in <300s, only 1.1% hit the 10-min cap. Spread reverts fast (median 17.9s), consistent with halflife=90s EWMA. No further gains from close_z or max_position dimensions.
iterations.next_action: Parameters now converged on: halflife=90, entry_z=1.0, close_z=-1.0, size=1000. Fine-tune entry side: sweep entry_z=[0.75,0.875,1.0,1.125,1.25] x min_spread_bps=[0.25,0.5,0.75] to see if lower spread filter or slightly different entry threshold can squeeze more trades or better edge.

iterations.hypothesis: Lower min_spread_bps filter allows more entry signals; finer entry_z grid may reveal better threshold than 1.0.
changes: Remote sweep: entry_z=[0.75,0.875,1.0,1.125,1.25], min_spread_bps=[0.25,0.5,0.75], all other params fixed at best
iterations.analysis: Marginal gains only (+3.7% PnL). min_spread_bps=0.25 beats 0.5 and 0.75 consistently — lower filter admits slightly more trades. entry_z=1.0 remains optimal even at finer resolution. Total PnL range across all 15 jobs is only 22.99–25.93 (<13%), confirming parameter convergence. Three consecutive iterations (6,7,8) show <5% PnL improvement — plateau reached on parameter space. Next step requires a structural code change: add an entry momentum/confirmation filter to reduce false entries where spread is still widening at entry.
iterations.next_action: Structural change: add entry confirmation filter. When z-score crosses entry_z, only enter if the z-score has peaked (current z < max z over last N ticks), confirming spread has started reverting. This should reduce adverse entries where spread continues widening post-entry. Try N=[3,5,10] ticks as sweep parameter.

iterations.hypothesis: Entry confirmation filter: only enter when z-score has peaked (current abs_z < max abs_z over last N ticks), ensuring spread has started reverting before entry. Should improve PnL/RT by avoiding entries at the top of widening moves.
changes: Remote sweep: reversion_ticks=[0,3,5,10], all other params at iter 8 best (min_spread_bps=0.25)
iterations.analysis: Entry reversion filter works: +6.6% PnL (25.93->27.64). Confirming that z-score has peaked before entry improves PnL/RT 9.2% (0.00472->0.00516) with minimal fill count reduction (21970->21450). The filter eliminates entries where the spread is still widening, ensuring we enter closer to the true peak. reversion_ticks=3 is optimal: 5 and 10 are nearly identical (27.44/27.43) suggesting the peak confirmation only needs 3 ticks of evidence. First >5% improvement since iter 5, breaking the plateau.
iterations.next_action: Fine-tune: sweep reversion_ticks=[1,2,3,4,5] x entry_z=[0.875,1.0,1.125] to find if reversion_ticks=1 or 2 might be better, and whether entry_z changes optimally with the filter. 15 jobs.

iterations.hypothesis: Fine-tune reversion_ticks=[1-5] x entry_z=[0.875,1.0,1.125] to find the best peak-confirmation window.

rev_ticks_sweep_at_entry_z_10.note: deque maxlen=1, always skips (current z == peak)

custom_metrics.catastrophic_flag: entry_z=1.125 + reversion_ticks in [3,4,5] yields PnL=-628 — false peak selection at higher threshold + longer window enters trades where spread continues widening

iterations.analysis: Only +0.6% improvement (27.64->27.81). reversion_ticks=1 gives 0 fills — the deque (maxlen=1) holds the current tick's z, so current always equals peak, filter always skips. reversion_ticks=2 beats 3,4,5: confirming peak over 2 ticks is sufficient. Critical bug discovered: entry_z=1.125 + reversion_ticks≥3 causes -628 PnL — the combination selects entries where a higher-threshold peak (1.125σ) appears to have peaked 3+ ticks ago, but is actually at a transient dip within a still-widening move. entry_z=1.0 + reversion_ticks=2 is the stable optimum. PnL improvement rate has flattened to <1% — parameter space exhausted. Structural changes needed.
iterations.next_action: Add stop_z (stop loss): exit early if the directed locked_z exceeds stop_z (spread widening beyond stop threshold post-entry). Sweep stop_z=[1.5,2.0,3.0] to cut adverse entries that would otherwise hit max_hold. Also test reverting the reversion_ticks=1 bug fix properly by only checking z BEFORE current tick.

iterations.hypothesis: stop_z parameter should cut adverse trades early; also tested with z_history timing fix (compute z before EWMA update).
changes: INCORRECT: changed z_history update order to pre-EWMA (caused all results to be invalid)

threshold_failures: ALL RESULTS INVALID due to z_history timing bug — comparing pre-update z in history vs post-update z in scan corrupts the reversion filter

iterations.analysis: Invalid run. The z_history timing change (computing z before EWMA update then comparing to post-update z in _scan_entry) caused apples-vs-oranges comparison, making the reversion filter fire at wrong times. All jobs produced strongly negative PnL. Reverted timing to original (update EWMA first, then compute and append z to history).
iterations.next_action: Re-run stop_z sweep with corrected code.

iterations.hypothesis: stop_z sweep retry with corrected z_history timing. Also first iteration using correct fee model (previous iters 1-11 had fee model bug: fees were ~3500x undercharged).

custom_metrics.fee_impact: CRITICAL: correct fees = $676.45 vs old buggy fees = $0.19 (3500x difference)

iterations.analysis: FUNDAMENTAL PROBLEM: strategy is not viable at correct fee levels. Correct taker fee = 4 bps × $80 notional/fill = $0.032/fill. With 21k fills, fees = $676 vs gross PnL = $28 (0.167 bps/RT). The 16 bps round-trip taker cost overwhelms the sub-1-bps spread edge. Stop_z with positive value actually INCREASES losses by generating more fills (stop-outs re-enter immediately). All previous iterations 1-10 were optimized against a zero-fee environment. USDC/USDE spreads are too tight for taker stat-arb at 4 bps. Must switch to maker (limit) orders to reduce fees from 4 bps to 1.2 bps per fill, or find a fundamentally different approach.
iterations.next_action: Switch to LIMIT (maker) orders for both entry and exit. Maker fees = 0.12 bps → round-trip cost = 4.8 bps vs 16 bps taker. With gross edge of 0.167 bps/RT, even maker fees are ~29x too high. Fundamental edge is too small. Try MUCH higher entry_z (5.0-10.0) to only trade the widest spread events — accept fewer but higher-quality trades. At extreme z-scores the spread reversion could be much larger.

iterations.hypothesis: With correct fees, the 90s halflife EWMA has zero gross edge at all entry_z levels (PnL/fill = -taker_cost at every threshold). Sweeping entry_z=[3,5,7,10,15] x close_z=[0.0,-0.5] tests if any taker threshold reveals profitable mean-reversion.
changes: Sweep entry_z=[3.0,5.0,7.0,10.0,15.0] x close_z=[0.0,-0.5] at correct fees

custom_metrics.gross_edge_conclusion: Gross PnL per RT = +$0.0018 (statistically zero) vs fee cost $0.129/RT at entry_z=15. PnL/fill converges to -$0.032 = exact taker cost at ALL entry thresholds. Zero gross edge confirmed.

iterations.analysis: DEFINITIVE: zero gross edge at all taker entry thresholds. PnL/fill = -$0.032 (= exact round-trip taker cost) regardless of entry_z. At entry_z=15, gross RT PnL = +$0.0018 vs fee cost $0.129 — 70x smaller. The USDC/USDE BTC perp basis on Hyperliquid contains no detectable mean-reversion signal at the 90s EWMA timescale. All fills are priced exactly at fees — no gross edge at any z-threshold. Taker stat-arb is structurally impossible. Must either: (1) switch to longer halflife (10min-6hr) to capture the correct timescale of basis mean-reversion, or (2) abandon this pair.
iterations.next_action: Test longer halflife to find the actual basis reversion timescale. Sweep halflife_seconds=[600,1800,7200,21600] x entry_z=[2.0,5.0,10.0] (12 jobs) — 90s may be measuring tick noise vs the 10min-6hr economic timescale of USDC/USDE basis dynamics.

iterations.hypothesis: 90s EWMA halflife is measuring microstructure noise. Longer halflives (10min-6hr) might capture the actual USDC/USDE basis reversion timescale.

custom_metrics.conclusion: PnL/fill = -taker_cost at ALL halflives. Zero gross edge confirmed across all EWMA timescales from 10min to 6hr.

iterations.analysis: Definitive confirmation: zero gross edge at all EWMA timescales. PnL/fill = -$0.032 (= exact taker cost) regardless of halflife from 600s to 21600s. Orderbook imbalance analysis on fills shows conf_imb does predict direction: high-imb quartile improves win rate from 23.66% to 34.69% over 10-tick horizon, and average reversion improves 73% (from -0.166 to -0.289 bps/10 ticks). But even with perfect filtering, gross edge is ~$0.005/RT vs taker fees $0.128/RT — 25x gap. Maker fees ($0.039/RT) still ~8x the gross edge. Taker approach is definitively unviable at correct fees.
iterations.next_action: Implement maker (limit) orders to reduce fees to $0.039/RT, combined with long-hold deep-reversion (halflife=1hr, entry_z=2.0, close_z=-2.0, max_hold=2hr) to capture larger gross edge per trade (~9 bps = $0.075/RT gross). Must use adequate warmup_ticks proportional to halflife.

iterations.hypothesis: Maker orders (limit) + 6hr halflife + 4hr hold + deep close_z=-2.0 should capture 4σ reversion ($0.075/RT gross) exceeding maker fees ($0.039/RT) = +$0.036/RT net.
changes: Added maker_orders=True, entry_fill_timeout fallback to taker

custom_metrics.bug: 6hr halflife with warmup_ticks=50 means only 16s of data → near-zero std → all 21 fills happen in first 4min 43sec. EWMA warmup insufficient for long halflives.

iterations.analysis: Failed due to warmup mismatch: 6hr halflife with warmup_ticks=50 (16 seconds) produces near-zero std estimate, making every tick a 10σ event. All 21 fills occur in the first 4m43s before the EWMA stabilizes. After that, no more entries. Need warmup_ticks proportional to halflife: for 1hr halflife, use ~10000 ticks (54 minutes) to get stable variance estimate. Mix of maker and taker fees confirms entry fallback triggered.
iterations.next_action: Retry maker orders with halflife=3600s (1hr) and warmup_ticks=10000 (~54 min of data before trading). This gives proper variance estimation. Sweep entry_z=[1.5,2.0,3.0] x close_z=[-1.5,-2.0,-3.0] x max_hold_ns=[3600s,7200s] = 18 jobs.

note: WARNING: best_pnl/best_sharpe from iters 1-10 are invalid — computed with severely undercharged fees (~3500x). True best PnL at correct fees: negative.
